records.ety: [OE. stampen; akin to LG. & D. stampen, G. stampfen, OHG. stampfōn, Dan. stampe, Sw. stampa, Icel. stappa, G. stampf a pestle and E. step. See Step, v. i., and cf. Stampede.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stamped (stmt ; 215); p. pr. & vb. n. Stamping.]

defs: To strike beat, or press forcibly with the bottom of the foot, or by thrusting the foot downward.
quotes: He frets, he fumes, he stares, he stamps the ground.

defs: To bring down (the foot) forcibly on the ground or floor; as, he stamped his foot with rage.
To crush; to pulverize; specifically (Metal.), to crush by the blow of a heavy stamp, as ore in a mill.
quotes: I took your sin, the calf which ye had made, and burnt it with fire, and stamped it, and ground it very small.

defs: To impress with some mark or figure; as, to stamp a plate with arms or initials.
Fig.: To impress; to imprint; to fix deeply; as, to stamp virtuous principles on the heart.
quotes: God . . . has stamped no original characters on our minds wherein we may read his being.

defs: To cut out, bend, or indent, as paper, sheet metal, etc., into various forms, by a blow or suddenly applied pressure with a stamp or die, etc.; to mint; to coin.
To put a stamp on, as for postage; as, to stamp a letter; to stamp a legal document.
To strike; to beat; to crush.
quotes: These cooks how they stamp and strain and grind.

defs: To strike the foot forcibly downward.
quotes: But starts, exclaims, and stamps, and raves, and dies.

records.ety: [F. estampe, of German origin. See Stamp, v. t.]

defs: The act of stamping, as with the foot.
The which stamps; any instrument for making impressions on other bodies, as a die.
quotes: 'T is gold so pure It can not bear the stamp without alloy.

defs: The mark made by stamping; a mark imprinted; an impression.
quotes: That sacred name gives ornament and grace, And, like his stamp, makes basest metals pass.

defs: That which is marked; a thing stamped.
quotes: Hanging a golden stamp about their necks.

defs: A picture cut in wood or metal, or made by impression; a cut; a plate.
quotes: At Venice they put out very curious stamps of the several edifices which are most famous for their beauty and magnificence.

defs: An official mark set upon things chargeable with a duty or tax to government, as evidence that the duty or tax is paid; as, the stamp on a bill of exchange.

A stamped or printed device, usually paper, issued by the government at a fixed price, and required by law to be affixed to, or stamped on, certain papers, as evidence that the government dues are paid; as, a postage stamp; a tax stamp; a receipt stamp, etc.
An instrument for cutting out, or shaping, materials, as paper, leather, etc., by a downward pressure.

A character or reputation, good or bad, fixed on anything as if by an imprinted mark; current value; authority; as, these persons have the stamp of dishonesty; the Scriptures bear the stamp of a divine origin.
quotes: Of the same stamp is that which is obtruded on us, that an adamant suspends the attraction of the loadstone.

defs: Make; cast; form; character; as, a man of the same stamp, or of a different stamp.
quotes: A soldier of this season's stamp.

defs: A kind of heavy hammer, or pestle, raised by water or steam power, for beating ores to powder; anything like a pestle, used for pounding or beating.
A half-penny.
Money, esp. paper money.
